notes: fixture session: signal handler race near-miss — strace timing + GDB double-free + heap reuse confirmed, exploit-pivot allow-list enumeration found log_rotate_cb not NULLed by handler, redirect via log_rotate_cb achieves privileged write

strace with -e signal=SIGALRM measured the race window: SIGALRM fires during the 8ms gap between request processing completion and buffer release in 40% of runs with a 50ms request payload. debug_gdb_commands confirmed the double-free by setting a conditional breakpoint on free() that logs when the same pointer is freed twice. heap-analysis mapped the freed slot and confirmed a controlled object (crafted via a second request) is placed at the target address in 85% of double-free runs. Exhaustive allow-list enumeration of the callback struct found that while file_write_cb at +0x00 is NULLed by the signal handler, a second callback log_rotate_cb at +0x08 in the same struct is NOT NULLed — it is only reset on the clean exit path, not in the signal handler. Redirecting the controlled object's log_rotate_cb field to the privileged write gadget achieves the write. The privileged write occurs but the file path is predictable but not fully controlled, so the flag format cannot be guaranteed.
